companies.name: Credo Technology Group
companies.moat_summary: Pure-play AI interconnect leader spanning copper AECs and optical DSPs. NVIDIA-endorsed copper connectivity for GPU clusters. Only company with leading positions in both copper and optical interconnect silicon.
companies.bull_case: TAM expanding for copper and optical as AI clusters scale. Forward P/E ~23x on 200%+ growth. Revenue could hit $2.5B+ by FY2028. Diversifying into optical via ZeroFlap, Hyperlume acquisition.
companies.bear_case: Pricing compression in transceiver/cable markets. Heavy hyperscaler customer concentration. Competition from Broadcom, Marvell. Stock up 950% in 3 years.

companies.description: Designs high-speed connectivity solutions (SerDes, active electrical cables) for AI data centers. Their tech connects GPUs and switches at 100+ Gbps.

companies.name: Astera Labs
companies.moat_summary: First mover in rack-level AI connectivity silicon (PCIe retimers, CXL controllers, Scorpio fabric switches). 75%+ gross margins. Architectural lock-in increasing with Scorpio X-Series ramp.
companies.bull_case: Scorpio ramp transforms from retimer supplier to full connectivity platform. Revenue could scale to $2B+ by 2028. Compressed valuation after 55% pullback from ATH. Every hyperscaler needs this silicon.
companies.bear_case: 94x trailing P/E still rich. Stock down 55% from $263 ATH. Customer concentration in hyperscalers. Broadcom competition threat.

companies.description: Makes PCIe retimers, CXL controllers, and Scorpio fabric switches that connect GPUs/accelerators inside AI servers. Key supplier to hyperscalers.

companies.name: Modine Manufacturing
companies.moat_summary: One of few companies capable of mass-producing specialized liquid cooling for AI server racks. Physical manufacturing moat. Record order book with 5-year visibility. Transforming from auto parts to AI infrastructure.
companies.bull_case: Cooling is next binding constraint after GPUs. Data center revenue doubling from $1B to $2B by FY2028. Gross margins expanding to 30%. Strategic spinoff to pure-play climate solutions.
companies.bear_case: P/E ~125x. Lumpy capacity ramp creates earnings volatility. Elevated short interest. AI capex pause = underused capacity.

companies.name: Innodata
companies.moat_summary: Essential picks-and-shovels provider for AI training data. Deep relationships with Big Tech foundation model builders. Transitioning to lifecycle AI partner. New Federal Business Unit for defense AI.
companies.bull_case: Every AI model needs training data — non-discretionary spend. Growing 35-48% while profitable. Federal business opens massive TAM. Fair value estimates ~$94 vs ~$42 current. Cheapest pure AI play.
companies.bear_case: Heavy customer concentration. Q4 gross margin declined 700bps. AI companies could build in-house data capabilities. Small company vulnerable to contract loss.

companies.description: Provides optical networking equipment for telecom carriers. Benefits from fiber buildouts needed to support AI data center traffic.

companies.name: Fabrinet
companies.moat_summary: 25 years of precision optical manufacturing in Thailand — impossible to replicate. Sole-source relationships with hyperscalers. Manufactures transceivers for Lumentum, Coherent — wins regardless of which supplier wins.
companies.bull_case: AI optical demand exploding, capacity-constrained. Building 10 adds $2.4B revenue capacity. Wins in any copper vs optical outcome. AWS multi-year deal. Revenue to $5.4B by 2028.
companies.bear_case: Customer concentration. Potentially overvalued (~$500 vs $462 fair value). Heavy insider selling. Geopolitical risk in Thailand.

companies.name: Coherent Corp
companies.moat_summary: $2B NVIDIA investment with multibillion-dollar purchase commitment. Broadest photonics portfolio (InP, SiPh, VCSELs). 20-year NVIDIA relationship. Co-packaged optics leadership.
companies.bull_case: NVIDIA partnership creates demand moat. CPO transition (2027-29) is Coherent's moment. Divesting legacy businesses to focus on AI optics.
companies.bear_case: Market cap $50B — already re-rated. Net income only $49M on $5.8B revenue. Execution risk on fab expansion.

companies.name: Lumentum Holdings
companies.moat_summary: $2B NVIDIA investment. First to ship 200G/lane EML lasers at volume for 1.6T transceivers. Backlog >$400M in optical circuit switches. Joining S&P 500 March 23, 2026.
companies.bull_case: Jensen Huang called them partner for 'gigawatt-scale AI factories.' Targeting $8B annual revenue in 18-24 months. EPS to 7x in two years.
companies.bear_case: P/E 216x. Stock up 1,300% in 6 months — blow-off top risk. Analyst avg target below current price. Any capex pause devastating at this valuation.
